attacking_style: Attacks 4-10km from summit on final climbs, exploiting lower GC positioning to escape favorites' watch. Prefers sustained tempo over explosive accelerations, timing moves when domestiques are exhausted or after race-altering events (crashes, shortened stages).
team_tactics: Frequently operates without deep team support on mountain stages, requiring solo bridging efforts. Quick-Step prioritizes sprinters or other GC riders (Alaphilippe, Fuglsang), forcing Martin into domestique roles or opportunistic breakaway hunting. Positioning errors suggest inadequate team protection in transition stages.
terrain_preference: Medium-length climbs (10-20km) with steady gradients where sustained power outweighs explosive acceleration. Struggles on steep ramps (9%+) against pure climbers and punchy finales requiring late kicks. Headwinds surprisingly suit his diesel engine style.
vulnerability: Poor positioning discipline in transition stages (crashes cost 1:14 and 1:43 in separate 2018 TDF stages). Exposed without team support when defending GC. Lacks explosive finishing power in uphill sprints when initiating too early. Cracks on sustained steep gradients (9%+) against elite climbers.
key_patterns: Peaks strategically within three-week races, improving to stage wins/top GC late (2020-21 evidence).
Solo bridging specialist: repeatedly chased through groups without support on mountain stages (2018 TDF multiple occasions).
Attacks when teammates exhausted or organizational chase lacking, exploiting power vacuums (4 instances 2017-2018).
Positioning errors in flat/transition stages: sitting too far back costs significant time (3 documented crashes/splits).
Initiates uphill sprints too early, lacks explosive kick when opponents respond (Catalunya 2017, Dauphine 2019).
Sacrificed for teammates despite GC capability when team hierarchy demands (Alaphilippe 2017 Paris-Nice, Lombardia)
form_trajectory: positive: 2021 Giro showed late-race peaking with stage win (solo 10km attack) and top-10 GC, pattern consistent with 2020 Vuelta 4th overall. Recent evidence suggests maintained ability to peak strategically in third week despite aging.
